data.title: Won't shut off
data.text: GOOD: Shower head, water volume, shower head pause button.BAD: Used it 5 times camping in September. While packing up, I heard it running in the shower tent! It had turned back on and it wouldn't shut off. Had to let the batter run & die. Now it's october and I completely recharged it for my husband to take up hunting. Turned it on and the water Pumps perfectly but as you'll see in the video I cannot get it to turn off. Had to use a screw driver to get down in there and turn it off, but when I tried it again the screw driver wouldn't even get it to turn off.  There is a short or something happening in there, but whatever it is, I am not pleased about spending the money on this.  Maybe it's just a one time thing that is not common, and I would be willing to try another one. Maybe.

data.title: It works well, when it chooses to work.
data.text: It works well, when it actually chooses to work. What typically happens is when I turn it off after wetting my hair, it will not suction and flow again after turning it back on. data.title: Battery Life is an Issue
data.text: Originally I got this for camping, thinking that I would pump it in one of those shower bags but upon receiving the pump I noticed that it was far too big to fit in any of them. That's okay, it will still work if you take a gallon bucket with you and use it that way. I also thought this heated the water when I got it, since the display changes colors but it does NOT. It just tells you how hot the water is, so that was a bummer.On to the review! So, my roommate and I only have one bathroom, and we tend to run on the same schedule. That includes somehow timing when we want/need to shower so I've washed my hair in the kitchen sink more than once. We don't have one of those hoses either so it's always been a literal pain in the neck doing it but necessary unless I want to look like a grease monster stepping outside. So filled up the sink and did my thing. The pump has some nice power! I really like the pressure. I would say it's as good as my normal shower head, if not a bit better. It's easy to adjust up or down. Pretty simple/stupid controls. + goes up - goes down. Power button turns the power on/off by holding 3 seconds. The instructions say it should be completely submerged but then say the lowest water level is the intake line, which makes sense. The pump comes with an extra filter, two ways to hang the shower head (suction cups or hooks). The pump also has 3 suction cup feet so it won't move on whatever surface be it bucket or sink. It's fairly light and comes with a bag for all the pieces to be stored.Now for the gritty, not nice. If you have the pump on when the water is below the intake, it will not bring water up even after you've refilled your (in my case) sink. I let the pump continue running while the sink drained of the soapy water, and was filling it back up with clean water. Even though the now clean water was now submerging the pump it would not pull water up the hose until I turned it off and then back on.Number two, I have some SERIOUS doubts about the 60-180 minute run time. Because I mistakenly thought it would heat the water I sat there.  From the start of my hair washing to while I waited about ten minutes the battery dropped from 100% to 80%. Given that, if I were using this as a full shower camping, I feel like I would only be able to get one shower (washing my hair, body, shaving) before I'd have to charge it again. And the instructions say a full charge can take 3-4 hours. The intent for this was to take it camping where there is no electricity and no functional showers. Just my parents generator that they run during the day.Value for money? It's about the same as other pumps currently on the market. It has good pressure. It's compact. If battery life isn't an issue for you, it's not a bad pump.

data.title: Gets the job done... barely
data.text: For a camp shower, this gets the job done. Barely. It works ok if you are not washing your hair. It will do the job if you have short hair. If you have long hair, don't even bother.Water output is low. You end up waiting to get wet, which actually causes you to use more water than normal, especially washing and rinsing hair.Pressure is low, and the water goes maybe 1 foot before it falls. That's why the shower head is angled down, because it wont' "shoot out" water at all. It works best when it can be pointed down, or close to down.The volume of water delivered is also low. The shower head is designed with only a few holes, so it seems like you are getting good flow... until you actually use it.The whole motor assembly sits in the water. I don't care how much you waterproof things, the motor is going to eventually get wet. It's nearly impossible to clean, and you have to pry off the lid to clean it. Bad design.The included mounting accessories are not super useful, for the most part you have to get creative with mounting.Battery life is excellent. The pump is lower power, and you can expect at least 5 showers on a charge.I've used this in a camp setting, in a pop-up shower enclosure. I have also used it in a home shower during a plumbing issue. Yes, it will get the job done, and it is better than nothing.  Ironically this camp shower uses twice as much water as it should because it takes far longer to shower than it should.If you plan on multiple showers during a longer camp outing, or need something for more than a single use, or want to use less water, this is not the best choice. For a zombie bug out bag... maybe.

data.title: Spray is not that strong

Better yet place the water reservoir about was high as your waist so the already weak motor/battery doesn't have to work so hard. You get a better spray as a result.Additionally, a Longer hose less efficiency.I trimmed the gray tubing going from the pump to the shower wand. This resulted a better spray pattern and better run time on the smallish batteries.Will not buy this again. I'll look for alternatives. data.text: 11/2022 Update:  Unfortunately the company has changed the design of the battery packs and the new style is difficult to remove and install on the camp shower. One more star removed from this review.Original review: I use this in my house call pet grooming business, to help mix and distribute shampoo and conditioner onto the pets, as well as rinse them.  I replaced the opaque hose with a clear hose (from local hardware store), and I don't use the sprayer nozzle (as it frightens the pets), I just wrap a rubberized hair band around the end of the hose to make it easier to grasp.  I like having the clear hose, as it makes it easier to make sure the hose is getting dry and not growing mold. I use it at least once or twice a week and it has lasted two years before one of the battery packs failed (it would be nice to be able to buy battery packs separately). The pump is quiet, and efficient at delivering the product/rinse water onto the pets.  I have bought 2 units to have spare batteries around, although one battery pack can usually last through one large dog.  I like to have back up chargers in my car and house, and back up batteries on hand in my grooming kit.  I don't leave the batteries out in the car heat and freezing weather, but bring them into the house to protect the battery life. One star off because I would really love it if they could make the black cord that attaches to the battery pack longer by about 12" (and a clear hose and housing and sprayer head would be awesome!) I actually link this product on my business Facebook page to recommend to my clients and friends for bathing their own dogs.  I have gotten feedback that it is also a great toy for the the grandkids, for washing cars, and camping!

data.title: No adjustment from 3.5 liter per minute flow rate. Pump is not self-priming.
data.text: This is a great rechargeable portable shower. It's nice that it has a base that is only 55 mm. It's small enough to fit some water jugs. The base contains a coarse filter, inlet, and the pump. The battery connects using a DC plug from a battery that's clipped onto the hose away from the base.One thing this can't do is adjust the flow rate from 3.5 liters per minute. It's either on or off. It would have been nice if there was a way to adjust the flow rate to conserve water and battery. What if you only had 3.5 liters to shower with? That's a one minute shower!You could cut into the wires and install a voltage regulator. By regulating the voltage that the DC motor sees, you can adjust the flow rate. This isn't a difficult or expensive modification to do.Also, the pump is not self-priming. Therefore, the pump needs to be submerged in water.While it's a great shower kit, I returned it for the lack of flow adjustment and because the pump is not self-priming. (There are some on Amazon that have these features.)

data.title: Gets the job done
data.text: In evaluating this product, I’m going to make 1.5 assumptions: that it is intended for use when no (heated) running water is available—otherwise, what’s the point of it? For instance, one chilly winter, I stayed in a Myanmar hotel with only cold water on tap. In the mornings, I was issued a bowl for scooping and a large bucket of hot water with which to wash. I can see a product like this being useful in such a situation. Indeed, my interest in this product stems directly from living in a hurricane prone area that has experienced water utility disruptions.First: does it work? Yes: it produces a low pressure, multi-stream spray cable of wetting one’s body and rinsing off soap/shampoo. Are there any limitations (other than the need for (hot) water and a way to charge its battery? Yes: according to the directions, the pump must be fully submerged—e.g., in a bucket—before it is turned on. However, inferring from the diagrams and the pump’s design (which sucks water in from its bottom), this is so one can purge all the air before turning it on, but that ensuring that it remain fully submerged through a usage cycle is not necessary (just don’t let the water level get so low that it starts sucking air though). [Note: it does not, nor does it claim to, heat the water—if you want hot water, you must provide your own.]I liked that the pump’s status panel shows both the battery charge and the water temperature; I also liked that it has a USB-C charing port (charger not included). I was able to take a quick shower using < 5 gallons of water and about 7% of the battery. The pump feels solidly built. The shower head looks and feels “cheap,” but it gets the job done.All that said, I’m not really seeing much of an advantage over a gravity driven “camp shower”—which has the advantage of not even needing to be charged. Maybe it’s better for people unable to lift a container of water up high?[Disclosure: I received this product for “free”—in quotes because the IRS considers the item’s retail value as taxable income for federal income tax purposes. This review is my personally written—not AI generated—honest opinion, and is uncensored by the seller.]

I will be marking the location of the power button on this shower , but with it being on the side and the side being smooth, it's a guessing game.Technically this one is supposed to be more 'powerful' but I can't tell any difference.I GREATLY appreciate it when a seller makes it easy to get support, Innhom provided contact information in the manual.

data.title: Amazingly Versatile Little Pump - So Handy!
data.text: -----------------------------------------------------Wish we had this in my old backpacking days!I bought this item when my hot water heater failed.  I brought an induction hot-plate into my bathroom and simply filled a large, clean container with warm water, dropped in the pump and showered!  And so versatile!... I had a bad leak in a kitchen faucet. To keep the kitchen functioning until I could make full repairs, the leak was being caught in a large container.  I was doing a LOT of bailing.  That got old fast!  This little pump came to my rescue!The pump can handle just about any small pumping (or bailing) job, or liquid transfer.It is incredibly small and light, and easy to charge and use.With a flow rate of just under 1 GPM and a pressure of maybe just under 1/2 PSI , you're not going to power wash your deck with it, but it worked great for the kind of chores I've described.  Run time is truly impressive.  I've put at least 50 gallons through it and the battery still shows no sign of weakening.  I'm still running on the first of two included rechargeable batteries.Like ANY pump, it can cavitate (push the air initially in it around in circles, instead of move the liquid), especially if the source reservoir is shallow, so you may have to turn it on its side to get the air out, and it helps to start with the outlet down near the liquid level until liquid flow starts (which you will feel and hear).  But possible cavitation is common to most any rotary pump, it is not a design flaw.I like it!

data.title: Got me through a tough couple months.
data.text: My water heater died on me in late November, leaving me with cold water for bathing. I could have replaced it with another tank heater, but I knew I was about to get a bonus at my job in January, so I decided to hold off until then and upgrade to a tankless gas system. This little gadget was half my workaround, the other being a submersible bucket heater. I'd fill my tub with around 8 inches of water, toss in the bucket heater, and let it sit for a couple hours. Once the temp hit about 105 degrees, I'd unplug the heater and take a shower with this thing while standing in a tub of hot water. It wasn't an amazing experience, but it was MILES better than an ice cold shower. This thing held up for more than 2 months of usage, and now that I don't need it I'm storing it away in case I need it again. May even use it for an outdoor pet shower when I wash my dogs.

data.title: The Best Camping Shower Except For...
data.text: Except for the missing pause button. Like what is found on most household shower heads. This is so good that it will go through 5 gallons of water in a few minutes.  As it is, you need to continuously turn off the pump at the battery to pause the water flow. The battery is not waterproof so that's risky, especially since the suction tube is very short compared to the shower hose. My solution is to leave the shower head submerged in the bucket  so that the water recycles. It has excellent pressure and you feel like you've taken a real shower. I would give it a 5-star rating if it had a pause function.

data.title: Works Great!
data.text: Absolutely love this shower so far! My husband and I have used this device for 1 week so far each taking a shower every  day. With this Corona virus thing we decided to take our RV to friends property to camp we have 30 amp power source but not direct water hook-up.  We can fill up a bucket of water to use inside our RV shower stall. Then use heated water for adding hot. We followed the easy instructions for hook-up. The pump was already charged when we got it and have only charged it once in a week. We used our vehicle charging port and a blue light comes on so you know when its charged. I wasnt sure how long the charge would last and certainly dont want to run out in middle of shower but even after 1 week with 2 showers a day only had to charge 1 time. A 5 gallon bucket works great filling 2/3 with well water then adding 2 tea kettles of Hot. We did figure out to fill the next person's bucket right away so it can get more room temperature but 1 time the 2 kettles of Hot was actually really a hot shower. Liked that the battery on off switch is separate out of the water and hooks onto the hose but it falls off sometimes so this is why the 4 stars. We just lean the hose over our existing shower nozzles and use 1 hand to hold device and rinse and turn it off and on during shampoo and washing. It has great water pressure too!  So far happy camping and nice warm shower feels so good . Will update as we are primitive camping for most of summer!

data.text: Bought this after moving to a new house. Due to the whole lock down deal we don't have gas service yet which means no hot water. So bought this to use with buckets of boiled water for hot showers. Been using it every day for over a month now and a week ago the battery that came with it died. Just won't charge at all and so taking off 1 star. It comes with a USB cable so you can power off power banks or the included 12V car plug. It doesn't work with a lot of power banks that I've tried. Eventually I was able to find 1 that worked so been using that. It probably tries to pull too much current on startup and the power bank's protection circuit cuts output. Definitely not as convenient as the included battery with button but does the job.Some people would rate this lower than 4 stars but honestly it works really well otherwise.

data.title: A life-saver during the aftermath of a hurricane...
data.text: This saved us during the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, so we did not have to always drive an hour or do sponge baths, when we could not use our water for over 6 weeks. We gathered water at the local well and lugged it to our place. We paired it with a heating wand, thanks to suggestions from our neighbor. It worked wonderfully! I hope to never have to use it again, as camping indoors is not fun, but we shall keep it for emergencies!

data.title: Verify it won't stay off. HOWEVER its fixed.
data.text: IMPORTANT UPDATE PLEASE READ REGARDING TURNING OFF.In my case the unit was STORED inside a container of water. That lead to its not staying off.  However once I let it dry for some time it functions properly - charging to 100%  and staying off.Why this would happen.  The container when filled with cold water that warms to at least room temp - let alone if in the sun to get hotter - exerts more pressure on the unit once the container is sealed.  That is just unavoidable physics.I don't recommend keeping the unit submerged in water between uses.*Because this did not ruin the unit and it still functions very well I changed my rating up to 4 stars.*Also because  the seller reached out to me on their own to solve the issue even offering to refund and replacement.quote from letter below."Now there is 2 available options for your reference:▲ How about we refund for this order to you?▲ How about we send a new replacement for you?"NOW THAT GIVES ME RESPECT.I decided to give their suggestions a try and I am happy to say they worked.CONCLUSION  I now highly recommend this unit. 1. because it took abuse and still functioned, 2 because the seller stands behind their product.OLD COMMENTI can verify this thing will not turn off, at least stay off.I went to use it and it was dead after only using down to 80% last shower.  Ok so I plugged it in for the night and camped out.  It woke me in the middle of the night vibrating on the counter.  I thought that was a way to tell it was charged. But, it only indicated 10%.  I pressed the off switch for 10 seconds when it tuned off, Leaving it I laid back down only to have it start back up. I tried 20, 30, 60 seconds and it keept turning on. This continues till it is dead again.What a shame for when functioning it works fine. Has good but slightly awkward controls. Its just the switch control as far as I can tell.

data.title: Nice Option for Festivals and Car Camping
data.text: Ordered this for car camping situations where we want to be able to shower, but aren't that concerned about weight and bulk of gear. For that, this is a great option. As long as you have a source to be able to fill up a bucket of water, it's very easy to use this for a basic shower. Definitely nicer than trying to wash hair and strategic areas using just a sink or stream. The pressure is selectable, and makes it easier to rinse hair vs. just splashing water with your hands or using a gravity shower. The charge lasted much longer than we expected -- we were able to get several showers on a single charge, and then could charge it back up using anything that can provide USB-C power to it.It's bulky, so definitely not something you would use backpacking or something where you're hiking your gear in. But, if for car camping situations it's definitely something we'll continue to toss into the trunk for multi-day excursions. At $32 with coupon at time of writing this review, it's a solid value to make a festival or other camping experience a little more comfortable.

data.title: GREAT FOR CAMPING AND QUICK CLEANUPS

data.title: Good Pressure and comes in handy!
data.text: As someone who spends a good amount of time outdoors, particularly with my paddle board, I'm always looking for practical solutions to stay clean and comfortable. This **portable camping shower** has proven to be an exceptional piece of gear that integrates perfectly with my watersports activities. The powerful 8000mAh rechargeable battery provides up to 180 minutes of continuous use, which is more than enough for multiple rinses after a paddle or even a proper shower at a remote campsite. What truly makes this shower stand out is its intelligent **LED display**, which not only shows the remaining power but also provides a real-time water temperature reading. While it doesn't heat the water itself, knowing the temperature allows me to gauge comfort and ensure I'm not getting an icy surprise. The adjustability of the water flow, with a convenient STOP knob and multiple gear settings, is also fantastic, allowing me to conserve water when needed or get a stronger rinse.The versatility and ease of use of this portable shower are also noteworthy. It comes with a **3-in-1 mounting kit** including hooks and suction cups, which makes it incredibly simple to set up just about anywhere – whether I'm hanging it from a tree branch, attaching it to the side of my vehicle, or securing it to my paddle board for a quick rinse. The pump itself is designed to stay firmly fixed at the bottom of any water container, preventing it from tilting or floating. Its lightweight and compact design, along with the included mesh organizer bag, means it easily packs away into my gear bag without taking up much space. For cleaning off my board, rinsing myself, or even washing my dog after a day at the lake, this portable shower works perfectly and has quickly become an indispensable part of my outdoor kit.

data.title: Good at the beach too
data.text: I got this for a few reasons. Camping, washing the dog, and washing dirt/sand off my feet before getting in the car at the beach. It works well in all those capacities. There are directions for use, and they are good ones. I would like to reiterate, though: Make SURE you cover the charge port tightly after charging and before immersing in water. I caught someone starting to drop the pump in water before doing this and made them check--it wasn't sealed. It has to be sealed. The directions say, at one point, to "push the air out of the hose;" just drop it in the bucket of water and turn it until the air is gone. Lastly, make sure you are using clean water--if using river water for example, pour the water through a pillowcase or something to filter out ant sand or sediment. I am quite happy with this portable shower. I can't attest to how long the battery lasts before needing a recharge as it hasn't run down yet. I do recommend it, though.

data.title: Notable kit
data.text: 180 minutes worth of pressurized showering water where none exists is pretty astonishing to me, and it’ll easily cover a weekend excursion. It’s powered by an internal 8000mAh battery and overall is a sweet package. It’ll display the temperature of the filled water, though it doesn’t heat it. I’m familiar with the gravity fed water pouch showers that hung in direct sunlight to heat the water up. The shower head on this unit will also hang from pretty-much anywhere or it can be suctioned cupped to the side of a vehicle or some other smooth surface. There’s three modes of water pressure/spray and all it needs is a bucket or body of water. It’s a lightweight carry (2lbs), and comes with its own mesh carry bag. Very nice kit.

data.title: Convenient, powerful, very helpful instruction manual and carrying bag
data.text: Small but mighty! I like that everything fits conveniently in the little mesh bag supplied. It makes it really easy to bring on camping trips or to a festival. I purchased it for festival, camping trips, and the suction cups would allow it to easily be mounted to a car for easy showering. Another excellent feature on this is that there are durable suction cups on the bottom of the device so that it doesn’t wiggle around or risk floating when putting into a bucket. The pressure controls work well. Mine came 80% charged but be sure to check yours if you intend to buy for a trip! I have it in a 1 gallon bucket in my video, for reference. When you turn the pressure up high, it empties pretty quickly so if you’re camping or at a festival, I would say bringing a 5 gallon bucket would make your life a lot easier. The charging port is protected by what seems to be an airtight rubber cap and my device was functional after.The box comes with a handy little instruction manual, which also contains troubleshooting for common issues that you may have with water, pressure and other things. I usually don’t keep these but in this case, it is handy to have and there is plenty of room in the bag for it!In my video, you will briefly hear the device squeaking, but it is only because the water was emptying from my 1 gallon bucket quickly. It is important that the device is fully submerged. I refilled my bucket a few times to make sure that there wasn’t any potential for a recurring water pressure issue, but that squeak was definitely my fault because I was running out of water!I live in an apartment without an outdoor hose hook up and after testing this outside, I think that in unintended use would be that I can use the high water pressure settings to help clear debris off of my porch, which is pretty cool!!!Highly recommend.

data.title: Quick and easy to setup.  It's well engineered and well made.
data.text: A perfect solution for when my apartment building has no hot water.  I keep this handy in my bathroom cabinet and use it whenever my building is working on the boiler and there's no hot water (which seems to happen too often).  I just put a big stew pot full of water on the stove for about 5 minutes.  When the water temperature reaches about 31c, I take the pot of water to my shower and drop this little water pump in.  It gives me plenty of water to soap up and rinse off even without pausing the water stream while bathing.  I love it.  And, I never have to be later to work because my building's lack of hot water.  It's perfect.

data.title: Great pump, lots of pressure
data.text: This is a great pump! We use this pump in a homemade water heater in our camper van. The water pressure is very strong. Having the battery/power switch outside the water is super convenient so I can easily turn the pump off and on without having to pull the pump out of the water. The small sprayer is convenient because it has an on/off lever that allows you to moderate, or stop, water flow without turning off the pump. The shower head is a nice spray but I'd like it better if it also had an on/off lever. I can't speak to longevity because we've only had it a month or so but so far, it's far exceeded my needs and expectations.
